boundaries.declared_end_rule: declared_end must match the referenced sidecar checkpoint head for checkpoint_id, chain_scope, sequence_number, and last_record_hash

canonical_filters.auditor_use: raw normalized filters are signed for auditor inspection; the verifier does not infer scope from hash-only filters
canonical_filters.filters_hash: sha256:<lowercase hex digest of canonical_filters.raw_filters canonical JSON bytes>

chain_evidence.disclosure_records: in-scope records presented as the declared sample or declared population
chain_evidence.proof_bridge_records: out-of-scope records supplied only to satisfy continuity; they must not count as scope members

declared_scope.presentation_policy_rule: the verifier adjudicates faithfulness to the declared scope with the declared presentation_policy applied; it does not adjudicate entitlement correctness

membership_reconciliation.cardinality_rule: signed matching_count must equal the number of supplied disclosure_records
membership_reconciliation.commitment_lookup: locate the sidecar scope_commitments[] entry whose predicate_value_hash equals the canonical hash of declared_scope.predicate
membership_reconciliation.range_rule: signed first_matching_sequence and last_matching_sequence must match the min and max disclosure record sequence numbers; for matching_count = 0 both are null and disclosure_records is empty
membership_reconciliation.root_recompute: recompute the deterministic Merkle root from supplied disclosure_records only using keel.scope_state.merkle.v1

relationship_to_export_integrity.manifest_only_rule: scope_faithfulness is not trusted merely because it appears in a manifest envelope
relationship_to_export_integrity.payload_binding: scope_faithfulness lives inside the exact export payload bytes covered by manifest.content_hash
relationship_to_export_integrity.precondition: export.integrity.v1 must support the export payload bytes and manifest before scope-faithfulness adjudication continues

scope_state_reference.artifact_hash: sha256:<lowercase hex digest of supplied sidecar artifact bytes>

segments.aggregate_rule: run once per scope_faithfulness.segments[] entry; aggregate export result is supported only when every segment is supported
segments.multi_chain_scope_rule: multi-chain-scope exports use multiple segments, one sidecar per segment
segments.single_segment_chain_scope_rule: a single segment that contains entries from multiple chain_scope values fails with EXPORT_SCOPE_CHAIN_SCOPE_MISMATCH
